detailed_analysis: slash_command_io.py is a Layer-3 interface adapter implementing the CustomizationTypeIO triple (parse/render/extract_pair_id) for the slash_command customization_type in both the Markdown+YAML-frontmatter dialect and the Gemini-style TOML dialect. It is structurally clean overall: no God Class (module-level functions only, no classes), every function is well under the 40-line limit, nesting never exceeds 2, there are no secrets, no injection vectors, no silent exception swallowing, and no mutable default arguments. The use-case isolation invariant holds (it imports only canonical/state/markdown helpers, no use-case modules) and no concrete tool name is hardcoded. The body of the command is passed through verbatim in both renderers (canonical.get('body', '')), so US-13 AC-2 byte-for-byte interpolation-grammar preservation is honoured. The defects that exist are all maintainability-grade: (1) a DRY violation — _toml_key and _toml_scalar are near-verbatim duplicates of the same private functions in codex_io.py, and the module also imports the private _normalize_toml_text from codex_io, an inappropriate cross-adapter intimacy that couples two sibling Layer-3 adapters; (2) several function names violate the project's two-word-minimum naming rule (_toml_key, _toml_scalar, _first_present); (3) the module is 310 lines, marginally over the project's 300-line soft limit. None of these are correctness or security risks. The strongest finding is the cross-adapter coupling/duplication, which is a maintainability and DRY concern (NFR-14 / Clean Code).

dependency_graph_summary.notable_coupling: Imports private symbol codex_io._normalize_toml_text — sibling Layer-3 adapter intimacy.

findings.problem: _toml_key (58-61) and _toml_scalar (64-75) are near-verbatim copies of the identically-named private functions in codex_io.py (lines 67-84). The only difference is that codex_io routes string quoting through its toml_string() helper while this copy inlines json.dumps. Two TOML scalar-emitters now drift independently; a fix to TOML escaping in one will not reach the other.

slash_command_io (a Layer-3 adapter for the slash_command type) imports the private, underscore-prefixed _normalize_toml_text from codex_io (a Layer-3 adapter for codex agents). Reaching across a sibling adapter for a private BOM-stripping helper couples two adapters that the architecture treats as independent gateways; a refactor of codex_io's internals silently breaks slash_command parsing. The BOM-normalisation concern belongs in a shared TOML/text utility, not in a per-tool adapter.

AGENTS.md §4 mandates a role+domain pair for names. _toml_key, _toml_scalar, and _first_present are single-concept names that read as utility labels rather than describing what they produce (e.g. format_toml_key, render_toml_scalar, first_present_value). _toml_dump_top_level (78) is acceptable. Minor, but a documented project convention.

The module is 310 lines, 10 over the project's 300-line soft limit (AGENTS.md §3). Borderline; the overage is largely the duplicated TOML helpers (CQ-01) — extracting those to a shared utility would bring it back under the limit. Not a true God Module: cohesion is high (one customization_type, two dialects) and there is no class.

findings.variant: TypeError raised on unsupported scalar — fail-loud (positive)
findings.problem: Noted as a positive: the scalar emitter fails fast and loud on an unsupported type rather than silently emitting bad TOML — consistent with AGENTS.md §8. parse_slash_command_toml (228) also fails closed with ValueError when the root is not a table (239). No action needed; recorded so the auditor's fail-fast scan is explicit.

notes: US-13 AC-2 (slash_command propagation; interpolation grammars preserved byte-for-byte): HELD. Both renderers emit canonical.get('body','') verbatim with no transformation of $ARGUMENTS / !-shell / @-file / {{args}} / !{cmd} / @{path} sequences.
FR-08 (slash_command matrix): the parse/render/extract triple supports the slash_command type for tools that register it; tool-agnostic.

US-10 AC-1/AC-4 (port contract, no concrete tool name, per_agentic_tool bags): HELD. agentic_tool_name is a parameter; per_agentic_tool_only / per_agentic_tool_extra are populated via canonical.apply_per_tool_partition. No tool name literal appears.

US-10 §5.1 rule 3 (extract_pair_id never raises on malformed input): HELD. extract_pair_id_from_slash_command_toml uses a regex .search returning None on no match; extract_pair_id_from_slash_command_markdown delegates to extract_pair_id_from_md.
US-10 §5.1 rule 4 (unknown fields not dropped): HELD via apply_per_tool_partition -> per_agentic_tool_extra.

NFR-15 / US-13 AC-5/AC-6 (secret_policy at egress): NOT APPLICABLE to this module. US-13 AC-5 states only the mcp_server adapter declares secret-detection heuristics (env/headers/auth.*); slash_command carries no such fields, so no egress secret check is owed here.
Architecture §8 I-3 (no destructive write without archive): NOT APPLICABLE. This module performs no filesystem writes; rendering/archive is the rendering.py gateway's responsibility.
Architecture Dependency Rule (§3): adapter imports inward only (canonical, state, markdown helpers) — no use-case import. HELD.

deviations.description: Clean Code maintainability: _toml_key and _toml_scalar (lines 58-75) duplicate the identically-named private helpers in codex_io.py (67-84), and the module imports the private codex_io._normalize_toml_text (line 20). 'Minimal duplication' and 'explicit boundaries' are violated: TOML scalar emission and BOM normalisation are shared concerns reached for across a sibling adapter rather than factored into a neutral shared utility. Soft consequence, not a correctness failure.

slash_command_io (Layer-3 adapter) depends on a private symbol of codex_io (another Layer-3 adapter). The module map treats each *_io adapter as an independent gateway depending only on canonical + shared IO helpers; reaching into codex_io's private _normalize_toml_text creates an undocumented adapter-to-adapter edge not reflected in the §4 dependency column. This is the same class of latent debt the doc records elsewhere (e.g. D-1/D-2) but is not itself listed as a known deviation.

verdict_summary: Clean, correct, secure slash_command adapter; sole real issue is cross-adapter TOML-helper duplication and a private import from codex_io (NFR-14 maintainability). code_score 0.75.
